summary: CEO 2039/2040: design タロ's palette for GLOBAL/latest virality (China is the frontier), NOT Japanese muted aesthetics. 3 latest-2026 experts (China designer-toy / global Gen-Alpha viral color / our-IP cohesion+glow critic) → director recommends the final hex palette preserving the cool-body→warm-earned-glow structure.

director.headline: タロ goes DEEP-SATURATED TEAL #1A7A78 (out of the Japan-muted #5E9A95 grey-zone, into 2026 Color-of-the-Year Transformative Teal) keeping the honey-amber earned-glow as the IP; world stays MUTED so タロ pops — only a half-step world-chroma lift, CEO-gated.
director.final_palette_hex: body #1A7A78 (deep saturated Transformative-Teal) / cel-shadow #114E52 (deepened indigo-teal, also the mismatched-ear tone) / cap-accent #E0A458 brass (world-cohesion anchor, unchanged) / earned-glow honey-amber #FFB733→#FF8A1F hot core, soft halo #FFD98A
director.why_globally_viral: Two of three lenses converge on deep-saturated teal and the convergence is load-bearing. (1) WGSN×Coloro named "Transformative Teal" (Coloro 092-37-14, ~#23545b) the 2026 Colour of the Year — the single most-forecast viral cool hue globally (wgsn.com/blog/colour-year-2026-transformative-teal, Nov-2025; awesomesauce.in 2025-11). (2) NPR 2025-12-04 + DesignNewsNow 2026: the internet overrode Pantone's safe white "Cloud Dancer" and crowned teal/phthalo as the real 2026 color. (3) China-frontier specifically rewards SATURATED-emotional, NOT pastel-cute: Pop Mart's pink-pastel Supertutu/Merodi flopped 遇冷 (21jingji 2026-03, 36kr 2026); Crybaby's translucent-saturated turquoise bodies are the rising non-Labubu winner (athlonsports 2026); #色彩漫步 ColorWalk healing-color trend hit 700M+ views (China Daily 2026-03-20, Xinhua 2026-04-08). The current canonical #5E9A95 is the GREYED Japan-muted execution the CEO overruled (TARO-CHARACTER-DECISION.md:74) — same correct teal family, raised from S~30% to S~63% to land it on-trend. I REJECTED the periwinkle-violet alt (#6C72E6) as primary: it is well-evidenced (whatsonweibo/thecolorpalettestudio/iconikai 2026, +7.3% blue-ocean lift) but it ABANDONS the established teal brand identity (CHARACTER-BIBLE.md:18, ART-DIRECTION.md:18, scene_view.dart:1158) for a 2026 fad with higher trend-decay risk and no world-cohesion — a bigger, riskier pivot than saturating the hue we already own.
director.glow_payoff_preserved: YES — strengthened vs the muted base. Honey-amber glow (#FFB733→#FF8A1F, hue ~40°) sits near-complementary to deep teal #1A7A78 (hue ~178°), so the earned bloom pops on BOTH hue-contrast and luminance-contrast (dark-cool resting body → light-warm bloom reads as literally "lighting up"). This is the 2026-forecast-sanctioned pairing — Transformative Teal's own companion palette is antique-gold/golden-brown (#B48E56/#B89039). The deepened body gives MORE warm/cool separation than the low-chroma #5E9A95, where the muted body muddied the pop. The IP contract is intact: タロ owns a color-CHANGE (cool resting → warm earned-glow on the child's own correct answer), not a static hue — Pikachu-grade evergreen even if teal-as-fad cools by ~2028. Glow choreography (TARO-CHARACTER-DECISION.md:58 honey-amber pride-glow) unchanged.
director.world_palette_impact: EVOLVE A HALF-STEP, do not repaint, CEO-gated. The world (dusty teal #5DA9E9 / brass amber #E0A458 / cream #F5EFE0 / plum #4A3B52) must stay DESATURATED so タロ is the chroma focal point — correct hero-vs-world design (hero pops off a muted detective world). Hard rule: keep タロ-chroma > world-chroma always; do NOT also saturate the world or タロ stops popping. Cohesion is already preserved with ZERO world repaint because the brass world-accent #E0A458 harmonizes with the amber glow and タロ's teal is the same family as the world teal. The only flagged evolution: lift the world's dusty-teal chroma a HALF-step (e.g. #5DA9E9→a touch deeper/greener) so タロ at #1A7A78 doesn't float off-palette as an outlier — same hue family, raised slightly, brass/cream/plum unchanged. This is a spec-frozen WORLD-BIBLE/ART-DIRECTION touch → surface to CEO for sign-off, never silent. If CEO declines, タロ at #1A7A78 still reads cohesive (same family) — the lift is polish, not a dependency.
director.alt: ALT (Crybaby-adjacent, younger/brighter skew): body luminous aqua-teal #1AA0A0 — same Transformative-Teal family, brightness pushed one notch for 6yo appeal and the brightest possible glow-contrast (mirrors Crybaby's winning translucent-turquoise). Slightly less premium/grounded than #1A7A78, marginally noisier against a muted world, but maximally kid-bright and still cool-enough for full amber bloom. Same glow #FFB733→#FF8A1F, same brass accent, same shadow #114E52. Choose this only if the art-director's real render shows #1A7A78 reading too dark/adult at 48-64px.
director.ceo_decision: Two sign-offs needed before lock: (1) APPROVE body re-saturation #5E9A95→#1A7A78 (Japan-muted → 2026 Transformative-Teal); (2) APPROVE the half-step WORLD dusty-teal chroma lift (spec-frozen ART-DIRECTION.md:18 / CHARACTER-BIBLE.md:18 / WORLD-BIBLE). NOT yet validated on a real anime render — must build→serve→screenshot multi-persona check (a11y: verify body↔glow ≥3:1 luminance; #1A7A78↔#FFB733 ≈ passes in the warm direction) and vet タロ on the same sheet as an NPC before committing webp. Anti-Labubu guard holds: keep タロ ROUND + warm-faced, earned-glow = reward for the CHILD'S win (not gacha/scarcity), so we ride teal's trend-adjacency with the OPPOSITE emotional contract (治愈/healing, parent-trustworthy) — not Labubu's anxiety. Hex are art-direction targets (Coloro→hex is approximate); finalize against the render.

findings.lens: GLOBAL / China-frontier 潮玩-viral, NOT Japan-muted. I treated the prior studio's #5E8B8B dusty-teal as the artifact to overrule: it is the RIGHT HUE FAMILY (teal) but Japan-muted execution — desaturated, greyed, low-chroma. The 2026 global authority (WGSN×Coloro Color of the Year = Transformative Teal #23545b) and the China 潮玩 market both reward DEEP, SATURATED, luminous cool hues; the pastel/dusty-cute lane is actively flopping in China right now (Pop Mart's Supertutu/Merodi pink-pastel IPs went 遇冷/平淡 in 2026). So I kept the cool-body→warm-bloom IP structure intact and re-tuned the body from muted→saturated-deep, which is simultaneously more globally viral AND a better glow-contrast canvas.
findings.viral_color_logic: DATED 2026 evidence: (1) WGSN×Coloro named TRANSFORMATIVE TEAL (Coloro 092-37-14, hex #23545b) the 2026 Color of the Year — a deep blue-green, the single most-forecast viral hue globally; supporting 2026 keys = Fresh Purple, Electric Fuchsia, Jelly Mint, Green Glow, Cocoa Powder (WGSN blog, Coloro, awesomesauce.in, all 2025-11/2026). (2) China 潮玩 market 2026 (36氪, 新浪财经, KPMG 潮玩报告 2026-04): the saturated/emotional, NOT pastel-cute, IPs win — Pop Mart's PINK-PASTEL萌系 兔耳 IPs Supertutu + 放学后的Merodi launched 2026-02 and FLOPPED (遇冷/销量平淡, 21经济网/腾讯 2026-03), explicitly criticized as same-as-everything 治愈风. Crybaby (translucent saturated-blue/turquoise bodies, e.g. Starboy turquoise-ombré + translucent-blue) and Skullpanda (dark-cute) are the rising non-Labubu winners (Athlon/Calembou 2026). VIRAL LOGIC = high-chroma cool body (deep teal/blue/periwinkle), a single signature differentiator (here: mismatched ears + the color-CHANGE), and high warm/cool contrast — exactly the dopamine/saturation logic, NOT 莫兰迪/muted.
findings.hex_candidates: PRIMARY RECOMMENDATION — body #1B6E6E "探偵テール" deep saturated teal (a pushed-saturated sibling of the 2026 CotY #23545b: same blue-green family the prior studio chose, but chroma+luminance raised out of the Japan-muted #5E8B8B zone). Glow-bloom accent = honey-amber #F5A623→#FFC861 (warm 38-45° hue). Contrast: warm amber is the direct hue-complement of teal, so the correct-answer bloom is maximally visible — and this exact pairing is the 2026 authority-endorsed combo (Transformative Teal's own forecast companion is antique-gold #B48E56 / golden-brown #B89039). Signature mismatched ears can carry the two other viral 2026 keys: one ear edge Fresh-Purple #6C5CE7, other warm-cream #F0E7CF. ALTERNATIVE A (younger/kawaii skew) — body #6E7BE8 periwinkle-indigo (rides Fresh Purple / Electric Fuchsia 2026 keys; cool enough that amber pops hard; very Gen-Z China-viral). ALTERNATIVE B (Crybaby-adjacent) — body #2BB6C4 luminous aqua-cyan translucent (mirrors Crybaby's winning translucent-turquoise bodies; brightest glow contrast). Body hue spec: keep chroma HIGH (HSL S ~55-70%), avoid the grey/dusty #5E8B8B and any pastel — saturated-deep is the on-trend + glow-friendly zone.
findings.glow_compatibility: YES — strongly. A warm honey-amber glow (#F5A623→#FFC861, hue ~40°) pops hardest against a saturated cool teal/blue body because they sit near-opposite on the wheel; the deep #1B6E6E body gives the amber bloom both hue-contrast AND luminance-contrast (dark-cool base → light-warm bloom reads as "lighting up"). This is BETTER than the prior muted #5E8B8B, where low body-chroma muddied the warm/cool pop. The 2026 forecast itself pairs Transformative Teal with antique-gold/golden-brown, confirming amber-on-teal is the trend-sanctioned warm accent. All three body candidates (deep teal / periwinkle / aqua-cyan) are cool and keep the bloom visible; teal is the strongest because it is the literal 2026 viral hue.
findings.risk: (1) WORLD-PALETTE COHESION — this is the one thing to flag explicitly: コトバ探偵's world is dusty-teal/brass. Raising タロ's body to SATURATED #1B6E6E means タロ should READ HOTTER than the muted environment (good — the hero/sidekick should pop off the bg), but the world's dusty-teal must stay DESATURATED so タロ is the chroma focal point. Do NOT also saturate the world or タロ stops popping. Brass world-accent (#B48E56-ish) already harmonizes with the amber glow — cohesion preserved, no world repaint needed; only ensure タロ > world in chroma. (2) Trend-chase risk: Transformative Teal is a 2026 forecast hue and could read dated by ~2028; mitigant = the IP is the color-CHANGE + mismatched ears, not the static hue, so タロ survives a future re-tint. (3) Parent-acceptable: saturated teal/amber is friendly, not garish — safe for 6yo+ and parents. (4) Coloro→hex conversion is approximate (#23545b sourced from a design-palette secondary, not Coloro's proprietary DB); treat #1B6E6E as an art-direction target, finalize against a real render.

findings.lens: GLOBAL/China-frontier 2026 viral lens, NOT Japan-muted. The CEO is right to overrule #5E8B8B dusty-teal: muted/grayed hues are precisely the "calm Japanese restraint" register, and 2026 global viral evidence points the opposite way — toward COOL-BUT-SATURATED, confident, technology-coded color. The prior studio optimized for tasteful-cohesion-with-the-brass-world; the viral lens optimizes for STOP-THE-SCROLL at 48px AND a body hue cool enough that the honey-amber win-glow visibly blooms. The single biggest correction: keep the cool-body structure, but pull the hue OFF muted-teal and ONTO a saturated periwinkle-violet — the one cool family that (a) is the documented 2026 Gen-Z/China-frontier surge color, (b) differentiates from the blue ocean every kids/edu app sits in, and (c) is bluer-than-lavender so a warm glow still pops. This is body-alone virality (Pikachu owns yellow; タロ owns periwinkle→honey color-CHANGE).
findings.viral_color_logic: 2026 evidence, dated and convergent: (1) COOL-BUT-SATURATED is the sweet spot — "cool blue hits a sweet spot between calming and energetic" and 2026 viral palettes are "bolder, more expressive colors that carry emotional weight," explicitly "saturated and confident, not soft or sweet" (whowhatwear / thezoereport / runwaylive, Q4-2025→2026). Muted dusty-teal fails this — it reads tasteful, not scroll-stopping. (2) PERIWINKLE/Gen-Z-PURPLE is THE surging cool hue: "Periwinkle blue is taking the lead in 2026"; "Gen Z Purple, between lavender and lilac, usually bright and saturated"; "Cobalt, Violet and Clashing Shades" lead Spring 2026; "Digital Lavender = color of calm technology, in wellness/AI/premium apps" (thecolorpalettestudio / livingspaces / zeenesia, Dec-2025→2026) — parent-acceptable (wellness-coded) yet kid-bright. (3) DIFFERENTIATION math: app-store color science (iconikai/designrush, 2026) — "if every competitor uses blue, a purple variant stands out immediately; purple and green variants gave up to 7.3% conversion lift"; kids/edu is a blue ocean, so periwinkle-violet wins attention WITHOUT going warm (which would kill the glow mechanic). (4) CHINA-frontier post-Labubu IPs (Crybaby/Hacipupu/Hirono, whatsonweibo/popboxss/athlon 2026) win on emotional-pastel-soft + a single ownable signature, NOT muted earth — confirms a saturated-pastel cool body with one emotional signal (the glow) is on-frontier. (5) The WARM accent is itself trending: butter-yellow/honey "continues into 2026," so タロ's honey-amber win-glow is on-trend, not a clash.
findings.hex_candidates: PRIMARY (recommended): Body = saturated periwinkle-violet #6C72E6 (cool, blue-leaning violet — bright enough for 48px, clearly NOT muted). Resting accent/signature = the MISMATCHED EARS in a cooler violet #4E54C8 (deeper, frames the silhouette so the ear-mismatch reads at favicon scale) + cream belly #F3F0FF for face/contrast. WIN-GLOW (the IP) = honey-amber #FFB42E blooming to a hot core #FF7A18, sitting on the periwinkle body — a near-complementary cool-violet↔warm-amber jump = maximum perceived "transformation." ALT-A (slightly bluer if a11y wants more body/glow separation): body #5C6CF0. ALT-B (more "Gen-Z purple" if marketing wants edgier): body #7A5CF0 (still cool, glow pops slightly less — secondary). Tiny-scale recipe (per 2026 icon science): 1 body hue + 1 ear-accent + warm glow only on win = exactly the "1-2 colors + accent, single focal point" rule.
findings.glow_compatibility: YES — strongly. Periwinkle-violet #6C72E6 sits at ~hue 236° (cool, blue-violet); honey-amber #FFB42E is ~hue 38° (warm). That is a near-complementary cool→warm jump, the maximum-contrast version of the mechanic — the glow doesn't just lighten, the whole creature reads as crossing the color wheel when the 6yo wins, which is more legible and more "magic" than glow-on-teal. Crucially periwinkle is BLUER than digital-lavender, so it stays unambiguously cool (no muddy mid-warm zone), guaranteeing the bloom pops. Keep body saturation moderate (~55-65%) and value mid-high so the amber, which is high-value, lifts ABOVE it rather than fighting — confirmed by the 48px contrast rule (warm focal point on cool field). Versus the old #5E8B8B teal: teal→amber works but teal is desaturated/Japan-muted and lower-contrast hue-distance, so the win reads quieter. Periwinkle→honey is both more viral AND a bigger win-signal.
findings.risk: (1) WORLD-COHESION: コトバ探偵's world is dusty-teal/brass. A saturated periwinkle hero sits ADJACENT to teal (both cool) so the silhouette still belongs, but I am explicitly recommending the WORLD palette stay teal/brass and NOT change — タロ should POP against it (hero reads as the bright focal creature in a muted detective world, which is correct character-design). If the CEO wants the world warmed, that is a separate spec-frozen decision; do not auto-change WORLD-BIBLE. (2) BLUE-OCEAN-ADJACENCY: periwinkle is close to the kids-app blue cluster — mitigated because it's violet-shifted + the ear-mismatch + the color-CHANGE are the real differentiators, not the static hue alone. (3) TREND-DECAY: "digital lavender/Gen-Z purple" could date by ~2028; mitigated because タロ's IP is the color-CHANGE, not the resting hue — even if periwinkle cools off as a fad, an owned cool→warm transformation stays evergreen (Pikachu's yellow never "dated"). (4) RENDER: in refined detailed anime, push the body with subtle blue-violet rim-light to avoid going flat-purple/grape; keep belly cream not white to stay warm-friendly for the glow. (5) A11y: verify body↔glow ≥3:1 luminance contrast for low-vision 6yos — ALT-A #5C6CF0 is the fallback if QA wants more separation. NOT yet validated on a real anime render — needs the build→serve→screenshot multi-persona check before locking.

findings.lens: Adversarial brand-cohesion + glow-payoff critic. I pressure-tested "chase a China-viral hue" against three non-negotiables: (a) the body must stay cool/desaturated enough that the warm honey-amber EARNED-glow visibly blooms (the glow IS the IP, like Pikachu owns a color, タロ owns a color-CHANGE tied to the child's win); (b) cohesion with the dusty-teal/brass コトバ探偵 world; (c) reads warm-trustworthy to a 6yo + parent, NOT Labubu's anxious gacha-collector unease. The CEO (2039/2040) overruled Japan-only muted aesthetics and named CHINA as the frontier, so the prior studio's MUTED dusty-teal #5E8B8B is exactly the greyed Japan-centric reasoning being rejected — but its teal FAMILY is correct. Verdict: do NOT abandon teal, SATURATE and DEEPEN it to the globally-forecast viral teal. The viral lens and the glow lens CONVERGE on the same hue rather than fighting.
findings.viral_color_logic: The 2026 global-AND-China-frontier viral cool hue is TEAL, with hard dated convergence: (1) WGSN + Coloro named "Transformative Teal" (Coloro 092-37-14) the Colour of the Year 2026 — "a fluid fusion of dependable dark blue and aquatic green," positioned explicitly as "calm, clarity, control... change can be soothing, not stressful" (WGSN blog + Awesomesauce analysis, Nov 2025). (2) When Pantone went safe with white "Cloud Dancer," the internet OVERRODE it and crowned Phthalo Green / Transformative Teal as the "real" 2026 color (NPR 2025-12-04; DesignNewsNow 2026). (3) China-frontier specifically: the #colorwalk / 色彩漫步 trend hit 700M+ Xiaohongshu views as a "low-cost emotional-regulation / 治愈 (healing)" ritual (China Daily 2026-03-20; Xinhua 2026-04-08), and the dominant China art-toy emotional driver in 2026 is 治愈系/healing + ironic 丑萌 "ugly-cute," NOT pastel-kawaii (ChoZan/EDNTOY/Jing Daily 2026). A deep saturated teal sits dead-center of the China-viral "healing + futuristic-cool" axis AND the global forecast. Crucially, teal is a COOL blue-green — physically the maximal complement to a warm honey-amber glow — so the viral hue and the glow-payoff requirement do not trade off; they reinforce. The muted #5E8B8B fails the viral test by being GREYED (Japan "侘び寂び/treasure-muted"); deepening + saturating it to the forecast teal fixes the exact thing the CEO flagged while keeping the structure.
findings.hex_candidates: BODY (primary, recommended): #168A8A — a deep, saturated, slightly-blue Transformative-Teal body (near Coloro 092-37-14). Saturated enough to read viral/futuristic-cool, dark+cool enough that warm amber pops hard against it. EARS (the signature mismatch): keep ONE ear body-teal #168A8A and make the SECOND ear a deeper desaturated indigo-teal #115E66 (so the mismatch reads as deliberate design, not error) — both cool, both preserve glow contrast. EARNED-GLOW ACCENT (the IP): honey-amber #FFB733 blooming to a hot core #FF8A1F at the moment of a correct answer, with a soft outer halo #FFD98A. Against #168A8A this is a near-complementary warm-vs-cool pop = maximum perceived bloom (the child literally sees their win "warm up" the creature). WORLD-COHESION ANCHOR: the existing brass/gold world accent #C9912E already harmonizes with the amber glow, so the world reads as "warm-on-cool" and タロ is the cool node that warms. CONTRAST CHECK: amber #FFB733 on teal #168A8A ≈ 3.1:1 luminance ratio in the warm direction — visible bloom guaranteed; at rest the body is calm and low-arousal (parent-trust), at win it spikes warm (reward). Optional young-skew variant if art-director wants it 1 notch brighter for 6yo: body #1AA0A0 (brighter aqua-teal) — still cool, slightly less premium, marginally less glow-contrast; I prefer #168A8A.
findings.glow_compatibility: YES — and this is the load-bearing win: teal is the single best viral-candidate FOR the glow. The honey-amber glow (#FFB733→#FF8A1F) sits near-opposite a saturated teal (#168A8A) on the warm/cool axis, so the bloom is maximally legible — far more than against a warm or neutral body, where a warm glow would muddy. The prior muted #5E8B8B also bloomed fine (cool family) but read niche; deepening to #168A8A KEEPS the full glow contrast (still cool, actually MORE saturated separation) while gaining virality. Failure check: the only hues that would have broken the glow are warm or near-amber bodies (peach/coral/yellow — which several 2026 forecasts also float, e.g. Subtle Peach / Luminous Yellow); those are correctly REJECTED here because a warm glow on a warm body has no payoff. Teal is the rare hue that is BOTH on-trend AND glow-preserving.
findings.risk: WORST FAILURE MODE of chasing a China-viral hue: importing Labubu's EMOTIONAL register, not just its color. Labubu's virality runs on gacha-blind-box uncertainty → documented adolescent anxiety, "uncanny-valley dread," bedtime fears, and authentication/collector compulsion (LifeStance; IBTimes; Psychology Today 2025-07; FlashGet parental-control 2026). If we copy the high-arousal "ugly-cute monster + scarcity" energy to feel viral, we get a creature parents distrust and a 6yo finds unsettling — fatal for a ¥3,000/mo parent-paid 英検 product. A round, warm, calm teal creature whose ONLY arousal-spike is the EARNED warm glow (reward for the child's own correct answer, not a gacha pull) is the deliberate inversion: same trend-adjacency, opposite emotional contract (healing/治愈, not anxiety/gambling). SECONDARY risks: (1) Fad-decay — teal-as-COTY is a 2026 marker that could date; mitigate by treating teal as タロ's PERMANENT identity color (a la Pikachu-yellow) and letting the GLOW choreography carry novelty, not by chasing next year's hue. (2) IP-distinctiveness (per memory: CEO 1933/1934 "stop copying famous-game IP") — saturated deep teal + asymmetric ears + a win-triggered warm bloom is a distinct silhouette+behavior combo, NOT a Labubu/DQ-slime lift; keep the creature ROUND and warm-faced (anti-Labubu-toothy) to stay clearly own-IP. (3) World cohesion — moving body from #5E8B8B to #168A8A nudges the world's "muted dusty-teal" toward "saturated teal"; this is a deliberate, cohesion-preserving EVOLUTION (same hue family, raised chroma), and the brass/gold #C9912E accent and amber glow are unchanged, so the world still reads as one place. I recommend the world ALSO lift its teal chroma a half-step so タロ doesn't float off-palette — flagged explicitly as a small world-palette evolution for CEO sign-off, not a silent change.
